mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals stronger economic growth, raising expectations of future corporate earnings and inflation, which lifts 10-year Treasury yields as investors demand higher compensation for growth and inflation risks over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between trailing GDP growth and 20-day yield change is ≤0 over a rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation pressure, which erodes real bond returns and prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, thereby pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If yield fails to rise in at least 60% of instances where the CPI acceleration trigger is met, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tightening labor market conditions, which fuels wage growth and inflation expectations, leading to higher 10-year Treasury yields as markets anticipate monetary tightening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over the latest 3 months
failure_condition: If yields do not increase with a mean yield change >0 within 20 trading days following the trigger, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) directly signals tighter monetary policy, causing short-term rates to rise and the yield curve to steepen, which lifts 10-year Treasury yields over the next 5 trading days as markets adjust to the new policy stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points from previous month
failure_condition: If yield change over 5 trading days after a FEDFUNDS increase is not positive in at least 70% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity and supply capacity, which supports economic growth and raises long-term yields as investors price in higher growth and potential inflation.
failure_condition: If the 60-day yield change is not positively correlated with trailing INDPRO growth over a 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts (HOUST) and retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicates robust domestic demand, which boosts growth expectations and inflation fears, driving 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 800 thousand units AND RSAFS > 350 billion dollars
failure_condition: If the average 20-day yield change following simultaneous trigger is not positive and significantly different from zero (t-test p<0.05) over a 10-year backtest, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve holds the federal funds rate near zero (accommodative regime), an increase in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is especially potent as it signals the end of the easing cycle, causing a sharp upward adjustment in 10-year Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the yield response to the CPI trigger while FEDFUNDS < 0.5% does not exceed the response when FEDFUNDS > 0.5% by at least 50%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of near-zero short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals genuine economic strengthening, prompting the market to expect eventual monetary tightening, which raises 10-year Treasury yields. In contrast, when FEDFUNDS is above 1%, rising INDPRO may already be priced in or signal overheating, leading to a negative yield response. For the current low-rate regime, the mechanism predicts a positive yield response.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change following positive INDPRO surprises (above median) in the low-rate regime is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p<0.05) or if the same signal in the high-rate regime does not produce significantly negative yield changes, the hypothesis is rejected.
